title: sida arboae
intro: Sida arboae, also known as the climbing sida, is a plant species belonging to the Malvaceae family, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Sida arboae include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of allergies and colds. Sida arboae can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can also be used as a poultice to treat skin conditions and wounds.
